Off Calendar Jun-25-2018 Granted Per Written Order

Off Calendar Jun-11-2018 Continued To Jun-25-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (25 Bradford St, San Francisco Ca)
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martha Daetwyler for Judith Matton.

Ms. Daetwyler stated that she has re-appraisal and a waiver of notice from Helen Lin. Ms. Daetwyler also addressed the requirement of Probate Code 10300 with respect to publication of notice stating that the date listed was an inadvertent error, that she put down the date the contract was signed and pointed out that over bidders were present in Court. The sale was confirmed at $1,360,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond is not required.

Judge: Lynn O'Malley Taylor. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Santiago. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Denied

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-07-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Kenneth Pacada.
Martha Daetwyler for Judith Matton (Sister of Decedent).

Ms. Daetwyler stated that Judith is the Sister and the only surviving sibling, that there is a Nephew and a Niece, that Judith is entitled to priority, that she is competent and quite capable, and that Judith has been paying the Decedent's bills since his death and that she brought the mortgage current. Mr. Lachona stated that his Client is better suited, that his Client was the Decedent's Partner of sixteen years, that he has knowledge of the finances and the assets, expressed concern about Judith's ability to be impartial and stated that Judith demanded his Client to leave the home. Ms. Daetwyler also stated that Mr. Pacada is in the category of a friend and there is no requirement to give notice, that her Client and the Decedent and Mr. Pacada maintained separate homes, that the Nephew and Niece approve of Ms. Matton, and that she has a waiver of bond from the Nephew and Niece. Mr. Lachona also stated that he believes a neutral fiduciary is appropriate and a $1,000,000.00 bond is appropriate. Both Petitions for Letters of Administrator are taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.
